title: Intentional binding for noninvasive BCI control.

assets.abstract: Objective. Noninvasive brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) allow to interact with the external environment by naturally bypassing the musculoskeletal system. Making BCIs efficient and accurate is paramount to improve the reliability of real-life and clinical applications, from open-loop device control to closed-loop neurorehabilitation.Approach. By promoting sense of agency and embodiment, realistic setups including multimodal channels of communication, such as eye-gaze, and robotic prostheses aim

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.
